dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to a stabbing at 1200-Block of Kinsley Rd.
Hello, I'm Mark, EMT with [VEHICLE_IDENTIFIER]. My partner is Rick from [UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER]. I need to ask you a few questions.
I was stabbed with a machete. I don't know why it happened.
Mr. Ramos, can you tell me your name and stay with me?
Opens eyes spontaneously, alert.
Mr. Ramos, I need you to listen carefully. Can you understand me?
Followed commands when asked to move fingers and toes. PMS is intact.
Airway is patent, and you're breathing on your own.
Breathing is equal on both sides. No sign of distress.
Respiratory rate is 24 per minute, not labored.
Blood pressure is elevated. Located an IV access site.
Blood pressure is 155 over 129.
Last respiratory rate was 24.
Heart rate is 102 now. Let's get you on the cardiac monitor.
Oxygen saturation is 97% on room air.
Breath sounds are clear on both sides.
Peripheral pulses are strong. Skin is warm with no pallor noted yet.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What happened before the attack?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: I was at a house party when a man came in with a crowbar and a machete.
Did you eat or drink before this happened?
I had chicken here a few hours ago.
Touch here-how would you rate your pain from 0 to 10?
This part of my arm and back is a 10.
Does it spread to your chest or neck?
No, it's just here and in the upper right back.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are round, equal, and reactive, about 4 mm.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Gross visual examination
dialogue.utterance: No signs of concussion or slurred speech.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Absence of jugular venous distension
dialogue.utterance: No jugular distension or tracheal deviation noted.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Absence of facial droop
dialogue.utterance: No facial droop or abnormal movements.
Pelvis is stable with no tenderness.
Abdomen is soft with no rigidity or distension.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Penetrating wound
dialogue.utterance: Wounds are on the right shoulder and over the upper back near the scapula.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Other injury sites
dialogue.utterance: A deep 3-inch laceration on the left posterior elbow, and a minor cut on the left thumb.
No complaints of pain or nausea.
Skin is warm, dry, but appears pale around the wound sites.
Bowel sounds are present and normal.
Pulse has dropped to 92 recently.
Breathing has increased to 26 per minute.
His blood pressure is now 82 over 42.
Still warm and dry. Some pallor near penetrating wounds.
Oxygen saturation is slightly improved at 98%.
Initiating 4 lead electrocardiogram monitoring now.
Applying an occlusive dressing to the right upper back wound to prevent air entry.
Controlled bleeding on the arm wound using 4x4 gauze and cling.
Blood sugar is stable at 167.
Establishing an 18-gauge IV in the right arm for fluid and meds.
EKG is showing sinus rhythm.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Absence of slurred speech
dialogue.utterance: Speech remains normal with no slurring.
No signs of respiratory distress.
[COMPANY_NAME] is the destination. Trauma alert was already sent.
He climbed in on his own and is safely secured.
IV is secured and fluids are running.
Monitor is active and readings are being taken.
Transporting emergent per protocol 4-1 due to penetrating trauma.
He's resting and calm now.
Ready to wheel him into the ER now.
Blood pressure is 82 over 42, SpO2 is 98%. Ongoing monitoring.
Readiness for handoff in the next few minutes.
Patient is stabilizing. No new concerns at the moment.
Trauma alert conveyed to [COMPANY_NAME] as the call is en route.
